headline: BCI Foods, Inc. Recalls Chicken Noodle Soup Product Imported without the Benefit of Import Reinspection
affected: 10.5-oz can of "tasty KITCHEN Chicken Noodle Condensed Soup PRODUCT OF CANADA" with best by date of "2028 FE 04" and lot code "5 9 2 26 035" · States: Oklahoma
why: Import Violation (FSIS Class I.) WASHINGTON, JULY 23, 2026 - BCI Foods, Inc., the importer of record located in Saint-Hyacinthe, Quebec, Canada, is recalling approximately 4,300 pounds of a chicken noodle soup product that was imported from Canada with…
action: Do not consume. Discard or return to the place of purchase. Check establishment number and dates in the official FSIS notice.

headline: Currey & Company Recalls Nottaway Chandeliers Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Electrocution Hazard
affected: Nottaway Chandelier Fixtures — About 447 units — Currey & Company showrooms, Wayfair and lighting stores nationwide and on Curreyandcompany.com, Wayfair.com and other websites from November 2025 through May 2026 for between $3,500 and $5,200.
why: The recalled chandeliers lack proper electrical grounding, posing a risk of serious injury or death from electrocution hazard.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled chandeliers immediately and contact Currey & Company for a free replacement chandelier, including shipping and installation at no cost to the consumer. Currey & Company will reimburse consumers for the full cost to remove the recalled chandelier and to install the new one by a licensed electrician and provide a prepaid label and package to return the recalled product.

headline: Huish Outdoors Recalls Hollis Diving Regulators Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Drowning
affected: Hollis 200LX Second Stage Diving Regulators — About 7,200 (In addition, about 370 were sold in Canada) units — Diving supply stores nationwide from May 2017 through February 2026 for about $330.
why: The regulator inlet tube can crack and break, posing a risk of serious injury or death from drowning.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled diving regulators immediately and return their regulator to the nearest authorized Hollis dealer for a free repair. Consumers can visit www.hollis.com/dealer-locator/ to find their nearest authorized Hollis dealer. Consumers can also contact Huish Outdoors for instructions on how to obtain a prepaid shipping label and return the product by mail for a free repair.
units: About 7,200 (In addition, about 370 were sold in Canada)
injuries: Huish Outdoors has received 17 reports of the diving regulator inlet tube breaking. No injuries have been reported.

headline: Jobon Torch Lighters Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Fire and Burn Hazards; Violate Mandatory Standard for Multipurpose Lighters
affected: Jobon butane torch lighters — 13,200 units — Online at Amazon.com and Alibaba.com from April 2021 through September 2025 for between $5 and $20.
why: The lighters violate the mandatory safety standard for multipurpose lighters because they do not have the required child-resistant mechanisms posing a risk of serious injury or death from fire and burn hazards. In addition, the lighters failed to meet the pre-market lighter submission requirement needed to demonstrate that the lighters feature child-resistant mechanisms and ensuring their safety and compliance with…
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled lighters immediately and contact Jobon Official Store for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to write "RECALLED" in permanent marker on the lighter, submerge it in water in a bowl and send a photo of the marked and submerged lighter to wzzhongbang@yeah.net. Consumers should then dispose of the recalled product in accordance with state and local waste disposal procedures.

headline: Liizousuda Paint Thinner Bottles Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Child Poisoning; Violate Mandatory Standard for Child-Resistant Packaging; Sold on Amazon.com by Shenzhen Gudebo Technology
affected: Liizousuda Paint Thinner — About 4,200 units — Online at Amazon.com from March 2025 through February 2026 for about $10.
why: The paint thinner contains low-viscosity petroleum distillates, which must be in child-resistant packaging, as required by the?Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The bottles are not child-resistant, posing a risk of serious injury or death from poisoning if the contents are swallowed by young children. Additionally, petroleum distillates can get into the lungs, causing chemical pneumonia and/or pulmonary damage, which…
action: Consumers should secure the recalled paint thinner out of the sight and reach of children immediately and contact Shenzhen Gudebo Technology for information on how to obtain a replacement bottle with a child-resistant closure, into which consumers should transfer the recalled bottle's contents.

headline: Madewell Recalls Women's Sweaters Due to Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Burn Hazard; Violate Mandatory Standard for Clothing Textiles
affected: Madewell Double V-Neck Pullover and V-Neck Cardigan Sweaters — About 5,900 units — Madewell and T.J. Maxx stores nationwide and online at Madewell.com from December 2024 through October 2025 for between $138 and $148.
why: The recalled sweaters violate the mandatory standard for clothing textiles, posing a risk of serious burn injuries or death.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled sweaters immediately and contact Madewell for a full refund in the original form of payment or store credit. Consumers will be asked to destroy the sweaters by cutting them in half and send a photo of the destroyed sweater to 24-7@madewell.com. Consumers should then dispose of the destroyed product.
injuries: The firm has received one report of the sweater catching on fire. No injuries have been reported.

headline: MNIENT Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Entrapment and Asphyxiation; Violate Mandatory Standard for Adult Portable Bed Rails
affected: MNIENT Adult Portable Bed Rails — About 330 units — Amazon.com from January 2026 through March 2026 for about $66.
why: The recalled bed rails violate the mandatory standard for adult portable bed rails because users can become entrapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the side of the mattress, posing a serious entrapment hazard and risk of death by asphyxiation. The bed rails do not meet structural stability or retention strap requirements, posing a fall hazard. Additionally, the bed rails' push pins and push pin hol…
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled adult portable bed rails immediately and contact Zhuzhoushizuisuanshangmaoyouxiangongsi for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to cut the foam padding on the bed rail handle grips, take a photo of the destroyed bed rail and email the photo to mnientrecall@outlook.com.

headline: Noerishia Adult Portable Bed Rails Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Entrapment and Asphyxiation; Violates Mandatory Standard for Adult Portable Bed Rails; Sold on Amazon by Hurzein
affected: Noerishia Adult Portable Bed Rails — About 504 units — Online at Amazon.com from March 2026 through April 2026 for about $100.
why: The recalled bed rails violate the mandatory standard for adult portable bed rails because users can become entrapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the side of the mattress, posing a serious entrapment hazard and risk of death by asphyxiation. In addition, the bed rails do not bear the required hazard warning labels.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled adult portable bed rails immediately and contact Hurzein for a refund. Consumers will be asked to cut off the gray foam handgrip padding and cut the securing straps in half, to render them unusable, then take a photo of the destroyed bed rail and send it to NoerishiaBedRailsRecall@outlook.com.

headline: Oitnlaughter Projecting Finger Light Toys Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Battery Ingestion; Violate Mandatory Standard for Toys; Sold on Amazon by Delightmi
affected: Oitnlaughter LED Finger Lights — About 1,012 units — Online at Amazon.com from July 2025 through April 2026 for about $14.
why: The recalled finger lights violate the mandatory safety standard for toys because they contain button cell batteries that can be easily accessed by children. If button cell or coin batteries are swallowed, the ingested batteries can cause serious injuries, including internal chemical burns, and death.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled toys immediately, take them away from children and contact Delightmi for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to properly dispose of the finger lights into the trash and send a photo of the disposed product to delightmi@outlook.com Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous. Batteries should be disposed of or recycled by following local hazardous waste procedure.

headline: SDADI Kitchen Step Stools Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury and Death from Entrapment and Fall Hazards; Imported by Yiwushi Bihe Trading
affected: SDADI Kitchen Step Stools, models LT01 and LT05 — 5,952 units — Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com, and Best Buy.com from April 2024 through September 2025 for between $45 and $155.
why: The recalled tower stools can collapse or tip over while in use, and a child's torso can fit through the openings on the front and back sides, posing a risk of serious injury and death due to tip over, fall and entrapment hazards.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled tower stools immediately, store them away from children and contact Yiwushi Bihe Trading for a full refund.
injuries: Yiwushi Bihe is aware of eight reports of instability or tip-overs, including four reported injuries such as scrapes, cuts, and bruises.

headline: Target Recalls Cat & Jack Children's Sandals Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Choking Hazard
affected: Cat & Jack Children's Sandals — About 211,000 units — Target stores nationwide and online at target.com from January 2026 through May 2026 for about $20.
why: The sandals' decorative pearls can fall off, posing a risk of serious injury or death from a choking hazard.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled sandals immediately, take them away from children and contact Target for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to return the sandals to any store or use a prepaid label to return the sandals by mail.
injuries: Target has received 23 reports of the shoe's pearls falling off. No injuries have been reported.

headline: TOMY Recalls Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Tower Kitchen Step Stools Due to Risk of Serious Injury and Death from Tip-Over and Fall Hazards
affected: Boon PIVOT Collapsible Toddler Tower Kitchen Step Stools — 116,908 (In addition, about 2,033 were sold in Canada) units — Online at Target.com, Amazon.com and Babylist.com from January 2023 through June 2026 for around $150.
why: The recalled toddler towers can tip over while in use, posing a risk of serious injury and death, due to tip over and fall hazards.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled tower stools immediately and store them away from children until repaired. Contact TOMY for a stabilizing repair part and installation instructions. The firm will mail the free repair parts directly to consumers.
units: 116,908 (In addition, about 2,033 were sold in Canada)
injuries: TOMY is aware of 11 reports involving the towers being unstable and shifting or leaning. No injuries have been reported.

headline: Wade Logan Annyka 9-Drawer Fabric Dressers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Tip-Over and Entrapment Hazards; Violate Mandatory Standard for Clothing Storage Units; Imported by Ningbo Jiangdong Peter International Trading
affected: Wade Logan Annyka 9-Drawer Fabric Dressers — About 180 units — Online at Wayfair.com from September 2023 through May 2026 for about $98.
why: The recalled dressers are unstable if they are not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards that can result in risks of serious injuries or death to children. The dressers violate the mandatory safety standard as required by the STURDY Act.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled dressers if they are not anchored to the wall immediately and place them in an area that children cannot access. Contact Ningbo Jiangdong Peter International Trading for instructions on how to identify affected units and dispose of the dressers to receive a full refund. Consumers will be asked to submit a photo to annykarecall@yeah.net demonstrating disposal of the product.

headline: Warren James Recalls the Copper Cup Found in Flashgitz Relic Lunch Boxes Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Lead Poisoning
affected: Space King No Girls Allowed Relic Lunch Boxes (Cups only) — About 7,000 units — Flashgitz shop from September 2025 through October 2025 for about $25.
why: The cup in the lunch boxes contains high lead concentration levels for drinking vessels, posing a lead poisoning hazard. Lead is toxic if ingested by adults or children and can cause adverse health issues.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled cups immediately and contact the firm for instructions on how to receive a replacement cup. Consumers who purchased the lunch boxes directly from Warren James will be sent a free replacement cup to the address previously provided in the original purchase order. Consumers should dispose of the cups in accordance with their local waste disposal requirements. The lunch box and o…

headline: Best Buy Recalls Insignia® Gas Ranges Due to Risk of Serious Injury from a Fire Hazard
affected: Insignia Front Control Gas Ranges — About 3,820 (in addition, about 700 were sold in Canada) units — Best Buy stores nationwide and online at www.bestbuy.com from November 2020 through March 2026 for between $280 and $1,470.
why: The recalled ranges' front-mounted knobs can be activated accidentally by humans or pets, posing a risk of serious injury from a fire hazard.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled oven immediately and visit Best Buy's recall website at https://www.recallrtr.com/range to participate in the recall. The firm is offering all consumers a free set of compatible knob covers with instructions for their gas range. The firm will send the knob covers to the consumers after they confirm that their gas range is involved in the recall. Consumers are cautioned to kee…
units: About 3,820 (in addition, about 700 were sold in Canada)
injuries: The firm has received one report of the knobs being activated by accidental contact. No injuries have been reported.

headline: Biometric Gun Safes Recalled Due to Serious Injury Hazard and Risk of Death; Sold Exclusively on Amazon.com by BBRKIN
affected: BBRKIN and MouTec Biometric Firearm Safes — 9,100 units — Amazon.com from March 2020 through February 2024 for between $260 and $409.
why: The biometric lock on the gun safes can be opened by unauthorized users, posing a serious injury hazard and risk of death.
action: Consumers should immediately stop using the biometric feature of the recalled safe, remove the batteries from the safe, and only use the key when storing firearms. Contact BBRKIN to receive a free repair kit.

headline: Conair Recalls Cuisinart Propel+ Four Burner 3-In-1 Gas Grills Due to Risk of Serious Injury from Laceration Hazard
affected: Cuisinart Propel+ Four Burner 3-in-1 Gas Grill with pizza oven — About 12,660 (In addition, about 83 were sold in Canada) units — Lowe's, Walmart and on-line at cuisinart.com from December 2024 through May 2026 for between $500 and $750.
why: The tempered glass window in the pizza oven can shatter during use, posing a risk of serious injury from laceration hazard.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled Cuisinart Propel+ Four Burner 3-in-1 Gas Grill immediately and visit Conair's website to check if their grill is included in the recall. If affected, follow the instructions to safely remove the tempered glass window on the pizza oven and upload two photographs to the firm's website; one of the removed glass, and one of the grill's serial number. Upon verification, consumers…
units: About 12,660 (In addition, about 83 were sold in Canada)
injuries: The firm has received 37 reports of shattered glass during use; one fire was reported. No injuries have been reported.

headline: Flaunt Recalls Magsafe Battery Chargers Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Fire and Burn Hazards
affected: Flaunt MagSafe Battery Chargers — About 1,400 units — Online at flauntcases.com from May 2024 through April 2025 for about $65.
why: The lithium-ion battery in the recalled power banks (chargers) can overheat and ignite, posing a risk of serious injury or death from fire and burn hazards.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled power banks immediately and contact Flaunt for a full refund of the $65 purchase price in the form of a check or as an $80 store credit at flauntcases.com. Consumers will be asked to register at www.flauntcases.com/pages/recall and submit two photographs. One photo should show the front of the power bank with the word "Recalled", the consumers initials, and the registration d…
injuries: The firm has received five reports of the power banks overheating and catching fire, including one report of a burn to a consumer's hand, one report of a burn to a consumer's arm and four reports of minor property damag…

headline: Greenworks Tools Recalls 24V and 48V Kobalt Yard Power Tools with USB-C Batteries Due to Risk of Serious Injury from Fire Hazard
affected: Kobalt 24V and 48V Trimmers, Blowers, Mowers, Chainsaws and Pruning Saws with USB-C Batteries — About 554,780 units — Lowe's stores nationwide and online at Lowes.com from January 2026 through May 2026 for between $20 and $482.
why: Charging the lithium-ion batteries through the USB-C port, while the batteries are inserted in the yard power tools can cause the batteries to short-circuit, posing a risk of serious injury from fire hazard.
action: Consumers should stop charging the batteries with the USB-C port while the batteries are inserted in the yard power tools immediately and contact Greenworks Tools to receive a free replacement battery. Consumers will be asked to complete the registration form at https://www.greenworkstools.com/pages/kobalt-product-recall to receive replacement batteries without the USB-C port, a charger adapter, a warning label to a…
injuries: The firm has received 34 reports of the batteries producing smoke, sparking or catching fire while the batteries are inserted in the tool and charging with the USB-C port. No injuries or property damage have been report…

headline: Moodooy Bed Rails Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Entrapment and Asphyxiation; Violate Mandatory Standard for Adult Portable Bed Rails; Sold on Amazon by Yuezhang
affected: Moodooy Adult Portable Bed Rails — About 228 units — Online at Amazon.com in February 2026 for about $38.
why: The recalled bed rails violate the mandatory standard for?adult portable bed rails?because users can become entrapped within the bed rail or between the bed rail and the side of the mattress, posing a serious entrapment hazard and risk of death by asphyxiation.?In addition, the bed rails do not bear the required hazard warning labels.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled adult portable bed rails immediately and contact YUEZHANG for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to cut the black safety strap in half, write "RECALLED" on the upper and lower bed rails with a permanent marker, take a photo of the marked bed rail with the buyer's name on a piece of paper, and email the photo to Moodooy-recall@outlook.com.

headline: WonderStone Infant Walkers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Fall Hazard; Violate Mandatory Standard for Infant Walkers; Sold on Walmart.com by Wonder Stone Toys
affected: WonderStone Infant Walkers — About 70 units — Online at Walmart.com in April 2026 for about $80.
why: The recalled infant walkers violate the mandatory standard for infant walkers because they can fit through a standard doorway and fail to stop at the edge of a step, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to a fall hazard.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled infant walkers immediately and contact Wonder Stone Toys for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to disassemble the walker, remove and cut the fabric seat, write "Recalled" on the top of the tray in permanent marker and send a photo of the disassembled infant walker showing the cut seat and marked tray to wonderstonerecall@outlook.com.

headline: AMASKY Nursing Pillows Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Suffocation; Violate Mandatory Standards for Nursing Pillows and Infant Support Cushions; Sold on Amazon by Pretty-Life
affected: AMASKY Nursing Pillows — 4008 units — Amazon.com from January 2026 through March 2026 for between $24 and $30.
why: The recalled nursing pillows advertised for infant feeding and tummy time violate the mandatory safety standards for nursing pillows?and infant support cushions because they can obstruct an infant's breathing, posing a serious risk of injury or death from suffocation.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled nursing pillows immediately and contact Pretty-Life for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to cut the pillow in half and send a photo of the destroyed pillow to amasky@126.com.

headline: Diamond Wipes International Recalls CVS Health Medicated Hemorrhoidal Wipes Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Child Poisoning; Violate Mandatory Standard for Child-Resistant Packaging
affected: CVS Health Medicated Hemorrhoidal Wipes — About 75,315 units — CVS and CVS Pharmacy stores nationwide and online at www.cvs.com from April 2020 through April 2026 for about $16.
why: The recalled medicated wipes contain lidocaine, which must be in child-resistant packaging, as required by the Poison Prevention Packaging Act. The packaging of the wipes is not child-resistant, posing a risk of serious injury or death from poisoning if the contents of the wipes are ingested by young children.
action: Consumers should secure the wipes out of sight and reach of children immediately and contact CVS for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to return unopened packets of wipes with the retail box packaging to any CVS store.

headline: Panasonic Recalls Electric Toaster Ovens Due to Shock and Fire Hazards
affected: Electric Toaster Ovens — About 11,480 (In addition, about 2,184 were sold in Canada) units — Online at Amazon.com, Costco.com, Panasonic.com and other online platforms nationwide from October 2024 through April 2026 for about $170.
why: The power cord insulation can be insufficient due to a protective fiberglass sleeve not covering it adequately, posing a risk of shock and/or fire hazard.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled Electric Toaster Oven immediately and contact Panasonic for a full refund.
units: About 11,480 (In addition, about 2,184 were sold in Canada)
injuries: There have been four consumer reports of tripped circuit breakers or outlets, and one report that the product stopped working. No fires or injuries have been reported.

headline: Cooper Lighting Recalls Metalux Optimized High Bay LED Light Fixtures Due to Fire Hazard
affected: Metalux Optimized High Bay (OHB) LED Light Fixtures — About 42,000 units — Cooper Lighting authorized distributors nationwide from April 2020 through May 2025 for between $425 and $950.
why: The LED's board can overheat or come into contact with the lens or nearby combustible materials, posing a fire hazard.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled light fixtures immediately and contact Cooper Lighting to schedule a free repair or replacement. Consumers will be asked to complete a form at www.content.cooperlighting.com/ohb-recall to register the light fixtures and schedule a repair or replacement.
injuries: The firm has received nine reports of the light fixtures catching fire. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

headline: Conair Recalls Over One Million Cuisinart Grill Brushes Due to Ingestion Hazard
affected: Metal Wire Bristle Grill Brushes — About 1,719,995 units — Burlington, TJ Maxx and Ross stores and online including Amazon.com and Cuisinart.com from June 2009 through March 2026 for between $8 and $20.
why: Small metal wire bristles can detach from the brushes and stick to the grill or food, posing an ingestion hazard and risk of serious internal injuries that could require surgery.
action: Consumers should stop using the recalled grill brushes immediately and contact Conair for a full refund or a credit for use at Cuisinart.com for a full refund plus 20% of the cash refund amount. Consumers will be asked to discard the recalled grill brushes.
injuries: Conair is aware of at least 54 reports and reviews where small wire bristles detached from the grill brushes, including three reports of consumers who swallowed metal bristles and sought medical treatment to remove the…
